newsArray.title: ‘Results weren’t good enough’: McCullum says he fully accepts decision to sack him
newsArray.description: Former Test coach apologises for poor results in last two years ODI series against India begins at Edgbaston on Tuesday Brendon McCullum has apologised for the flatlining results that triggered his removal as England’s Test head coach but has promised to collaborate with his successor as leadership of the men’s national teams is once again split. Speaking before the first one-day international against India on Tuesday – a series that remains under his remit as head coach of the men’s white-ball sides – McCullum showed no resentment regarding what he called “the tap on the shoulder”. Continue reading...
creator: ali martin

newsArray.title: Shubman Gill Emphasizes Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's Role Ahead of ODI World Cup
newsArray.description: In a recent press conference, Indian captain Shubman Gill praised the pivotal roles of Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli in the team's batting lineup, calling them the backbone of Indian cricket over the past decade. As India prepares for the 2027 ODI World Cup in South Africa, Gill highlighted the need to explore new player combinations. The team is looking to bounce back from a disappointing T20I series against England, aiming for a fresh start in the upcoming ODI matches. With a star-studded lineup, including Kohli and Sharma, India is determined to improve their performance in the 50-over format.
creator: narendra chaudhary

newsArray.title: India Women create history with first-ever Test win at Lord’s, beat England by 270 runs
newsArray.description: India Women defeated England by 270 runs to register their first-ever Test victory at Lord’s, with Yastika Bhatia’s century and Kranti Gaud’s five-for leading the way.
creator: aritra chatterjee

newsArray.title: 'Auction Will Mark Defining Moment': ACA President Ahead of Inaugural Assam Premier League
newsArray.description: Guwahati: The Assam Premier League auction is set to be held on July 19 at the 91 Yards Club inside ACA Stadium, where 275 cricketers from across the state will be drafted by eight franchises ahead of the tournament’s inaugural season. The league is scheduled to begin on August 1, marking a major step for organised franchise cricket in Assam . According to an official release, the player pool includes a marquee list of 20 cricketers representing the top tier of Assam’s domestic circuit. Among the prominent names are Denish Das, Sibsankar Roy, Sumit Kashyap, Swarupam Purkayastha and Jitumoni Kalita, who form the core of the batting and all-round categories. The bowling lineup is led by Mrinmoy Dutta, Muktar Hussain, Avinav Choudhary, Rahul Singh, Sadak Hussain and Bhargab Pratim Lakhar. These players are expected to attract significant bids as franchises assemble their squads for the first edition. Assam Cricket Association President Taranga Gogoi said the auction will mark a defining moment for the competition. “The auction on 19 July is the day this league becomes real for our players. Cricketers from every district of Assam have put their names forward, and in a week they will know which of the eight teams they will represent,” he said. Mr. Taranga Gogoi, President of the Assam Cricket Association, shares his thoughts ahead of the historic auction for the inaugural season of the Assam Premier League 🙌 #Auction #AamarAxomAamarGourab #APL #Season1 #AssamCricket pic.twitter.com/zIKqm8tovq — Assam Premier League (@aplt20_) July 13, 2026 He added, “When the first match is played on 1st August, we want every fan in the state to feel that this league belongs to them. Assam has waited a long time for a platform of its own, and we intend to make the first season one to remember.” The broader player list also features Saahil Jain, Pradyun Saikia and Saurav Mousum Dihingia, strengthening the batting options. All-round capabilities will be provided by Abdul Ajij Kuraishi, Akash Sengupta and Ayushman Malakar. For wicketkeeping roles, Sumit Ghadigaonkar, Rohit Sen and Ruhinandan Pegu are among the key names. All 20 marquee players have represented Assam in BCCI tournaments, making them central to franchise strategies during the auction.
creator: pratidintime sports desk

newsArray.title: 'Very Proud Moment For Us': Shubman Gill Congratulates India Women's Team After Historic Lord's Win | VIDEO
newsArray.description: India captain Shubman Gill addressed the women's team historic win over England at Lord's in the one-off test match in his press conference before the IND vs ENG 1st ODI. India defeated England by 270 runs in a monumental victory, earning plaudits from the cricketing fraternity, including Gill.
creator: sreehari menon
